diff --git a/CONTRIBUTING.md b/CONTRIBUTING.md
index 14f5c44..e47585c 100644
--- a/CONTRIBUTING.md
+++ b/CONTRIBUTING.md
@@ -89,6 +89,8 @@ again.
 
 The most reliable way to build and test Guava is:
 
+**Minimum requirements:** Maven 3.8 or newer and JDK 8 or newer.
+
 ```shell
 ./mvnw clean install
 ```
diff --git a/pom.xml b/pom.xml
index 91fe4da..7887af6 100644
--- a/pom.xml
+++ b/pom.xml
@@ -172,10 +172,10 @@
             <configuration>
               <rules>
                 <requireMavenVersion>
-                  <version>3.0.5</version>
+                  <version>[3.8.0,)</version>
                 </requireMavenVersion>
                 <requireJavaVersion>
-                  <version>1.8.0</version>
+                  <version>[8,)</version>
                 </requireJavaVersion>
               </rules>
             </configuration>

exploration_log.aha_moment: Maven version range syntax [3.8.0,) means 'at least 3.8.0, no upper bound', and [8,) is the standard way to express 'Java 8 or later'. Both are Maven Enforcer range expressions.

key_decisions.decision: Use range syntax [3.8.0,) and [8,) instead of exact version strings
key_decisions.reasoning: Task explicitly specifies '[3.8.0,)' and '[8,)' as the target values; range syntax also prevents exact-match false rejections on newer versions

solve_notes: Updated `requireMavenVersion` from `3.0.5` to `[3.8.0,)` and `requireJavaVersion` from `1.8.0` to `[8,)` in the `maven-enforcer-plugin` execution in `pom.xml`. Also added a 'Minimum requirements: Maven 3.8 or newer and JDK 8 or newer' line to the Building and Testing section of `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
task_text:
Fix pom.xml maven-enforcer-plugin requiring outdated minimum Maven version

The parent `pom.xml` configures a `maven-enforcer-plugin` execution
with a `requireMavenVersion` rule requiring `3.0.5` and a
`requireJavaVersion` rule requiring `1.8.0`. These thresholds are
dangerously low: Maven 3.0.5 predates multi-module reactor ordering
fixes that were resolved in 3.8+, and accepting any Java 8+ JVM
means the build may silently succeed on an unsupported JVM. Update the
`requireMavenVersion` version to `[3.8.0,)` and the `requireJavaVersion`
version to `[8,)` in the parent `pom.xml` enforcer execution. Also
update `CONTRIBUTING.md` to document the resulting minimum Maven 3.8
and JDK 8 requirements for building.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set (the two pom.xml elements and the CONTRIBUTING.md section) is already everything an agent needs. These are configuration values with no dependencies on other code definitions. There are no sibling patterns or external references that would cause an agent to search elsewhere.
